chunk_summary: This section discusses strategies for building iron-dense vegetarian weaning foods in India, focusing on culturally appropriate dietary practices, iron absorption enhancement, and anemia prevention during complementary feeding (6 months to 2 years).
learning_objectives: Understand the dietary challenges of iron absorption in vegetarian weaning foods.
Learn strategies to enhance non-heme iron absorption using vitamin C-rich foods.
Recognize the role of milk management and therapeutic iron in preventing anemia.
Identify key nutritional interventions for vegan families, including vitamin B12 planning.
key_facts: The peak age for nutritional iron deficiency anemia (IDA) in India is 6 months to 2 years.
Limit milk intake to 500–600 mL/day in children aged 1–5 years to prevent displacement of iron-rich foods.
Pair non-heme iron sources (e.g., dal, legumes) with vitamin C-rich foods (e.g., citrus, guava) to enhance absorption.
Therapeutic iron should be given 1 hour before or 2 hours after milk/dairy for better absorption.
In vegan families, immediate vitamin B12 supplementation is critical to prevent deficiency.
reference_quote: The peak presentation age for nutritional IDA in India is during complementary feeding (about 6 months to 2 years), when diet structure determines iron intake.
document_key_topics: Vitamin B12 planning for vegan families
chunk_heading: VEGETARIAN & REGIONAL FEEDING PATTERNS;BUILDING IRON-DENSE VEGETARIAN WEANING FOODS;
